programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings throw a surprise birthday party for one of the clan. When Happy Birthday rings out, the surprise is as great for Grizzy, who is trying to take a nap after a hearty meal, as it is for the birthday Lemming hero in question.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A gust of wind sweeps away the flower that Grizzly's adored she-bear wears in her hair. Grizzly sees this as a golden opportunity to dazzle her by bringing it back.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds two identical vases that are actually communicating vessels in a Native American Indian chest. If he sticks his hand in one, it comes out the other, regardless of the distance between them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a vintage stopwatch in the garage which lets one wind back the minute that has just passed by, and it can even stop time and movement in one's surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is disturbed in the middle of his TV snack by a flow of incongruous objects that have been transformed into hot-air balloons.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers with great delight that the ranger has purchased a brand new multi-function Stressless recliner. But the Lemmings transform it into a fearsome multi-game chair.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play kite surf with a homemade kite. Grizzly notices that the she-bear he's in love with watches the kite perform arabesques in the sky with admiration.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy snatches the last jar of chocolate spread out from under the noses of the Lemmings. In exchange, he offers them the toy stuck inside the lid, a mini-flute.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play a new game, floating like flags in the breeze from the fan set on maximum. But Grizzy quickly rids the living room of this interference.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have a grand old time tearing down their Kapla block buildings, which they rebuild just as fast. Grizzly remarks that the she-bear he's in love with is impressed by the Lemmings' construction and makes believe that it's his work of art.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings draw inspiration from lessons they find in a martial arts book and start breaking every wooden object they can get their hands on into two. When Grizzy tries to stop them, they go on to the next lesson and get him in a judo hold.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dreams that he's a valiant knight who has delivered his beloved. As he is about to take her in his arms, he is woken by the Lemmings who have chosen this day to blast music.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzy imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: At the Ranger's home, Grizzy finds a high-tech gadget that enables you to learn anything within seconds thanks to electrodes hooked to the brain, meaning that he can become the perfect bear.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Back in Pallet Town, Ash, and friends meet Florinda, who works at the local nursery. Her Gloom won't evolve, so she’s afraid she's not training it properly. When Team Rocket attacks, everyone sees just how strong a Pokémon can become!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Everyone wants to become the star when a famous director arrives in town! Ash, Brock, Misty, and Team Rocket are working hard to ensure their Pokémon will be the ones to star opposite Wigglytuff in his movie as he solely desires them.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When the gang attends the premiere of 'Pokémon in Love,' Meowth looks back on his painful past. He shares the story of his life before Team Rocket, including how he learned to walk and talk like a human.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: With only three weeks left before the Pokémon League competition, Ash, Brock, and Misty head up to Mt. Hideaway to find one of the Elite Four. They're after Pokémon training tips, but they discover a land of overgrown Onix instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: While Ash and Brock train for the Pokémon League, they uncover a hidden temple containing Pokémon fossils. Misty remembers a hidden city where people built temples to honor Pokémon. Could they have stumbled upon the ancient city of Pokémopolis?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes are headed to the Pokémon League tournament at the Indigo Plateau when they meet another Trainer going their way. Unfortunately, all of his Gym badges have been stolen, so he can't compete…unless he takes Ash's badges instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: The Pokémon League is about to begin with the ceremonial lighting of the torch, which carries a flame from the Legendary Pokémon Moltres. Of course, Team Rocket tries to steal it!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: It's the first day of the Pokémon League, and when Ash's friends learn his first battle will be held on the Water Field, they're full of advice!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: As the Pokémon League continues, Ash battles on the Rock Field and has a run-in with Team Rocket after they set up a fake Pokémon Center to lure unwary Trainers!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash faces his toughest opponent yet on the Grass Field at the Pokémon League! Are he and his Bulbasaur ready to take on his opponent and her powerful Scyther?

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.desc: Three outlaws worth $15,000 in bounties prove hard to count for part-time census takers Rowdy, Pete and Wishbone.

programme.title: Rawhide

programme.title: Rawhide

programme.desc: A former cop is convicted of killing her husband's ex-wife. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Saving Emily
programme.desc: When her daughter is diagnosed with leukemia, a woman (Alexandra Paul) and her new husband get pulled into the whirlwind life of her seedy ex-husband who tries to extort money from them in exchange for his life-saving bone marrow.

programme.title: Mary Higgins Clark

programme.desc: A young orphan girl, Sophie, gets taken away to a faraway land populated by Giants and Dreams.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Old Dog, New Tricks
programme.desc: Bryan Sr. resurrects an ancient crane dubbed Rust Bucket to complete a remote fishing cabin. Meanwhile, Beat struggles with two huge projects competing for time and square footage on the work yard.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Cold War
programme.desc: In Kansas, tensions run high when Andre muscles in on Sam’s big build. And back at the log yard, winter gales put a chill on Peter’s enormous project for a Russian client.

programme.title: Timber Kings: A Big Deal

programme.title: Timber Kings: Pig in Mud
programme.desc: In the Prairies, Peter gets stuck in the mud, trying to make a client’s dreams come true. Meantime, Beat helps Sam with his biggest build ever, but a reckless rookie crane op threatens to bring the whole project down.

programme.title: Timber Kings: High Rollers
programme.desc: Just hours from Manhattan, Beat creates a country retreat for a demanding French financier, while Joel’s Australian client makes a last minute request for a three-car garage.

programme.title: Timber Kings: Trial By Fire

programme.title: H.G. Wells' The Invisible Man: The Big Plot
programme.desc: Found among the wreckage of a crashed plane are parts of an atomic bomb and Peter uncovers a plot to start a Third World War.

programme.desc: A psychologist is stabbed to death in a seemingly sealed room. Inspector March must find who killed him and how did they accomplish the task.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: At Night All Cats Are Gray
programme.desc: A lady claims an odd man is following her and fears for her life. She’s later found murdered...in mismatched color, a telling clue for the inspector.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Case of the Kidnapped Poodle
programme.desc: Colonel March investigates a valuable stolen diamond as well as the kidnapping of a little boy's poodle; he thinks both incidents may be connected.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Invisible Knife
programme.desc: March must solve the mystery of how a man could be stabbed to death by an invisible knife, while attempting to summon The Devil during a black rite.

programme.title: Colonel March of Scotland Yard: The Strange Event at Roman Fall
programme.desc: A wife reports her husband, a reclusive mystery writer, has fallen off a cliff. Suspicion points to her as the killer and Colonel March investigates.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Twins who are unaware of each other's existence are murdered.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A man incriminates himself when he falls in cement while trying to transport a dead body.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Catherine and Nick investigate a case in which a guilty man was proven innocent.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: The team is joined by a new CSI, Michael Keppler, who assists them on a case.

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Catherine reluctantly agrees to help Keppler deceive the team in order to catch a murderer

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Grissom is sent a miniature crime scene in a cardboard box to solve before it happens.

programme.title: Scorpion: Gator Done
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to the bayou to track down a cagey alligator in order to stop a deadly mosquito-borne virus, and Paige teaches Walter the concept of the 'white lie.'

programme.title: Scorpion: Foul Balls
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must use their high IQs to win a softball game when the homeland director challenges, with all of their future government contracts on the line.

programme.title: Scorpion: Kenny and the Jet

programme.title: Scorpion: A Lie in the Sand
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to Northeast Africa where they must carefully navigate a minefield in order to save the lives of local villagers.

programme.title: Scorpion: Pilot
programme.desc: When a crisis at LAX leaves dozens of airplanes unable to land, the government enlists a team of misfit geniuses to save the day.

programme.title: Scorpion: Single Point of Failure
programme.desc: The Governor calls on Team Scorpion when his daughter is mysteriously "bio-hacked" with a deadly virus and has only 24 hours to live.

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Ninety Degrees
programme.desc: Andy heads out with a very-pregnant Marlo to find a volatile woman who’s gone missing after group therapy, but neither Andy nor Marlo are prepared when Marlo suddenly goes into labor.

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Breaking Up the Band
programme.desc: Now that Sam and Marlo’s baby girl has arrived (Ep. 520), it’s clear nothing could have prepared Andy for this moment. So when Juliet offers Andy an opportunity to leave town for 4 months on an undercover project, Andy starts to consider it.

programme.title: Rookie Blue: 74 Epiphanies
programme.desc: Andy and Sam’s wedding comes just in time for the gang to celebrate one last hurrah. But when Andy is taken off-course by a hitchhiker and then stranded on the side of a dead-end road with a dying man, she finds herself with no way to get help

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Fresh Paint

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Mercury Retrograde

programme.title: Dinoshark

programme.title: Safe

programme.title: Wild Card
programme.desc: When a Las Vegas bodyguard with lethal skills and a gambling problem gets in trouble with the mob, he has one last play and it's all or nothing.

programme.title: The Bargee

programme.title: The Big Job
programme.desc: A gang of crooks are caught on a big job. 15 years later they find their loot is now buried under a police station.

programme.title: Crooks Anonymous
programme.desc: When petty thief Dandy Forsdyke is coerced into going straight by his stripper girlfriend, he joins an unofficial organisation for reforming criminals.

programme.title: Make Mine Mink
programme.desc: An Englishwoman recruits a retired major, a spinster and a teacher to steal furs for charity.

programme.title: Rookie Blue: All by Her Selfie
programme.desc: Oliver gets promoted and Andy is tasked with training 15 Division’s newest rookie, Duncan Moore. But when Andy and Duncan respond to a messy homicide at a pawnshop, it’s clear this rookie is going to give Andy the challenge of a lifetime.

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Heart Breakers, Money Makers

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Wanting
programme.desc: When a tenement-building shooting leads to the discovery of an executed gang leader, 15 Division must race to find the killer before a gang war breaks out

programme.title: Rookie Blue: Going Under

programme.title: Monkey
programme.desc: Shotenki and his demon master Kokufki lure men from nearby villages to their casino in Ittakudo, where gambling consumes their lives and fortunes.

programme.title: Monkey

programme.title: Monkey

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: Deadly Women

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: My Ghost Story
programme.desc: A medium experiences the torture of a prison snitch killed by other inmates. Two soldiers in Iraq are startled by ghostly encounters in abandoned bunkers; and a police officer witnesses paranormal activity in a deserted spa.

programme.title: My Ghost Story
programme.desc: A couple is haunted by the spirit of a little boy who lives in their young son's bedroom closet. A gas station owner's life is abruptly changed by the appearance of a mysterious paranormal mist with a demonic presence.

programme.title: My Ghost Story
programme.desc: Two men retire without knowing their new home sits on the graves of people who were tortured and murdered by the KKK. The Queen Mary once ruled the high seas, but now ghosts roam her decks.

programme.title: My Ghost Story
programme.desc: A woman under assault by an aggressive entity is rescued by the spirit of her deceased son. A maid who was murdered in the 19th century continues to work at an old Texas hotel.

programme.title: My Ghost Story
programme.desc: The suburban house of two young cousins is filled with scary encounters after a demonic ceramic giraffe is brought into their home.

programme.title: My Ghost Story

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: Mortal Passion

programme.desc: Deborah finds herself in a fairytale romance with bad boy Steven. But after they marry, Deborah becomes a subject to daily abuse. When she leaves him, Steven stops at nothing to get revenge.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Labor of Love
programme.desc: Harley Davidson V-twin EVO engine and revolutionary 1958 dual-suspension swingarm frame; '80s Ms. Pac-Man arcade machine; rare Air Jordan vintage shoe collection in mint condition.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Flying Ton, Creeping Allen
programme.desc: When a group of thugs try to bully Ton and Allen out of a Chinatown auction, the guys fight back, leading to a violent showdown and the discovery of both a rare Bornean blowgun and special effects wire harnesses.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Missouri Loves Company
programme.desc: On a visit to see Ton's sister in Missouri for some rafting and relaxation, the guys hit up a fast-paced live auction. After some tough losses, Ton and Allen score a ballista and an anvil set made for an explosive sport.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Half Pipe Dreams
programme.desc: 1970s Z-Flex Jimmy Plumer Poolrider skateboard with artwork by Doug Smith; Kevlar bulletproof vest, ballistic facemask and a helmet used by S.W.A.T. teams; a pair of 1990s racing pocket bikes capable of going 80 mph.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Brew-Phoria
programme.desc: Ton and Allen go all in for an electric car, but with the batteries missing, a pricey refurb is their only shot at a profit. Will a home brewing system and high-end air rifles help soften the blow, or will they take a big loss?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: You Got Served
programme.desc: After a crushing defeat, the guys leave an auction empty handed and scramble to find new merchandise at a yard sale. But questionable antique tennis rackets and an ancient special effects box may not earn them a cent.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hidden Hot Wheels
programme.desc: Ton and Allen challenge a big time auction buyer on his home turf. The guys uncover a 1969 Chevelle Malibu muscle car and a single-shot pistol designed to aid Allied resistance fighters during WWII.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hula Moola

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Night of the Digging Dead

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: The Conspiracy Show travels to New York, LA and Toronto in order to investigate the murder of John Lennon. Several researchers posit that Lennon's supposed killer Mark David Chapman was either a patsy or a mind-controlled assassin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Did Jim Morrison Fake His Death?
programme.desc: Is it conceivable that the Doors legendary front man did not die in a bathtub in Paris of an apparent drug overdose?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Water Engine
programme.desc: Did the late Stanley Meyer figure out a way to run an internal combustion engine on pure water? And was he murdered because of the threat his invention posed to Big Oil?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Did this pop icon and blond bombshell succumb to a drug overdose in 1962 as we've been led to believe or was she killed for 'knowing too much?"

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Is The Cure for Cancer Being Suppressed?
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the medical profession, drug companies, the food industry and government agencies are suppressing a cure for cancer.

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: UFOs: The Best Evidence Volume 1 - The Visitors
programme.desc: This volume of UFOs the Best Evidence focuses on The Visitors, traces UFOs throughout history, including sightings by the world’s most famous people, and an astounding collection of UFO photos and videos which seem to defy explanation and science.

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: Touched
programme.desc: When Harvard psychiatrist John Mack approached filmmaker Laurel Chiten asking her to make a movie about encounters with aliens, she thought he was crazy. But after meeting some of the so-called experiencers she was intruiged.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1983, Graeme Cunningham vanishes in Salt Lake City. Detective Don Bell discovers Arthur Bishop, who confesses to killing Graeme and four other boys. Bishop is found guilty in 1984 and executed in 1988.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Waterspouts
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, George uses a motorboat, a van and small plane to get up close to waterspouts - the nautical cousins of tornadoes. He also meets some of the 25 million alligators that live in the Sunshine State.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Seasons of Saturn
programme.desc: Saturn is the second largest planet and has an astonishing 60 moons in its orbit. Thanks to the Cassini mission, it's also one of the most studied and most photographed planets in our neighborhood.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Virtual Universe
programme.desc: Our understanding of the cosmos is limited by the amount of time we have to see it. Fortunately, computer simulations can help us see how galaxies interact over millions of years, and even how dark matter structures the universe.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Vesta Unveiled
programme.desc: With the Dawn missions exploration of the asteroid Vesta and the dwarf planet Ceres, asteroids are a hot topic. See what these incredible missions have uncovered.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Stargazer's Paradise
programme.desc: The Atacama desert is a perfect place for watching the night sky. With still winds and only a handful of cloudy nights each year, it allows scientists to get the absolute most out of their tools.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Hot Zone
programme.desc: Mercury is one Earth's closest neighbors, but for centuries it eluded astronomer's best efforts to learn its secrets. But that has changed thanks to missions like Mariner and most recently, Messenger.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Hubble's Renaissance
programme.desc: Before we can reach space, we must first be able to see it. Hundreds of years ago, the simple telescope was used to give us a seemingly closer vantage. Today, we use the awe-inspiring, and super powerful, Hubble Space Telescope.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Science and the Shuttle
programme.desc: For more than 25 years the shuttle was our ticket to space, and our laboratory in orbit. Uncover the legacy of this amazing machine, and see what lies ahead.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: The key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists give true insider's perspective to the space race of the late 1960's.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: Munchkin finds a new purpose after the loss of her pups and Swift’s leadership is in question.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Red Desert
programme.desc: In this episode of Wild Places, we join botanist Peter Latz, who believes the biggest influence on the Australian arid landscape has been fire. Years ago, Peter wondered how different this country would have looked like without the influence of it.

programme.title: Wild Spain: The Atlantic North
programme.desc: From the Basque Country to Galicia: This is the North Spanish wilderness.

programme.title: Wildest Places: Australia
programme.desc: Wildest Places explores some of the most incredible natural habitats on earth & a diverse range of wildlife.

programme.title: Wild Germany: Rivers and Lakes
programme.desc: Water is the beginning of all life - including the wildlife of Germany. The episode Rivers and Lakes takes the viewer on a journey through the water world of Wild Germany.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: The Britannia Bridge
programme.desc: Rob Bell recounts the story of the Britannia Bridge.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: The Clifton Suspension Bridge
programme.desc: The story of Brunel's famous bridge over the Avon gorge.

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges: The Humber Bridge
programme.desc: Rob Bell tells the story of the Humber Bridge.

programme.title: Concorde: Part One
programme.desc: How Concorde became the world's only supersonic airliner.

programme.title: Concorde: Part Two
programme.desc: By the early 1980s, Concorde was admired the world over.

programme.title: Great British Royal Ships: Her Majesty's Yacht Britannia
programme.desc: A vessel that has been a floating palace and a rescue vessel.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Bourdain looks at the Congo as one of the more industrialized powers in Africa now largely crumbled to nothingness and chaos with Tim Butcher author of Blood River.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Detroit holds a unique place in American history. Few cities have seen such a dramatic rise and fall from economic boom to disparity. Tony explores the past, present and future of the Motor City.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: For the first time, Tony visits Budapest, 25 years after democracy planted its flag –alongside the Oscar-winning master of light, cinematographer Vilmos Zsigmond, as one of his key guides.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: In this episode of Parts Unknown Tony explores a few of the many faces of Miami. Chef Michelle Bernstein takes Tony out to an authentic Cuban breakfast and a late-night dinner at a gas station café in Doral.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: After eight years, Tony returns to South Korea. And this time, he’s no stranger to kimchi. But just as Tony has changed, so has Korea.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony explores the other Hawaii, the one that those eight million tourists don’t see when they come to the islands. He discovers the fabric of a true cultural mixing pot.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Lost in Paradise
programme.desc: Dog lectures high school students about the dangers of drugs before going after Joseph, a dangerous fugitive who won't be easy to capture.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Coaching Day
programme.desc: Dog takes the day off to try his hand at coaching, and assigns Tim and Leland to track down a dangerous fugitive on their own. Can the boys prove they can handle it?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Women of Waikiki
programme.desc: The boys get distracted by the bikini-clad lovelies on Waikiki when they hunt for Lucy, a homeless, alcoholic fugitive. Beth is amused by the boys, but this is serious business: Tim will have to pay the bond if Lucy isn't caught.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: To Capture One's Own
programme.desc: Dog faces a tough personal and professional situation when he must hunt down his friend, Ili, who has helped him on many busts. But now Ili's on the other side of the law, and now Dog must bring her in.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Big Bags and Boxers
programme.desc: Dog is out to make two grabs on two islands in one day. Part of the team is goes to Kona to nab a woman who skipped her court date. Meanwhile, Dog and the others hunt Patrick, a fugitive who's fighting in an Extreme Boxing event on the Big Island.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Cats & Dogs
programme.desc: Dog has only a few days to catch a woman nicknamed Cats or forfeit Beth's $10,000 bond. But it won't be easy - Cats is eight months pregnant, so Dog will have to lure her in rather than risk a violent grab.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Destiny Love
programme.desc: Dog is in for a big surprise after he catches a fugitive named Jonaven in a sting operation.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: If You Knew Nunu
programme.desc: Dog and the team really have their hands full when they go after a tough guy named Nunu, who leads them on a chase through apartment complexes and a shopping mall before he's finally captured.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Double Trouble
programme.desc: Dog's feeling lucky, so he's going to try to capture two women in one day. The first, Moani, has "crazy" tattooed on her neck and is facing drug charges. The second fugitive, Jade, who turns out to be a tough catch, despite help from Jade's sister.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Big Island, Small Town
programme.desc: Dog has problems when he goes to a small town on the Big Island of Hawaii to track down a fugitive. In a small town, everyone knows everyone else's business, making it impossible for Dog to hunt in secret.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Samurai Si
programme.desc: Willie receives a special gift from a Japanese business associate - a "priceless" samurai sword, which Willie keeps out of the hands of Jase and the guys.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck No, We Won't Go
programme.desc: Willie pushes his work force too far after implementing new "motivational work tactics," and the guys rise up against him and go on strike.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Drag Me To Glory
programme.desc: When NASCAR sensation Clint Bowyer pays a visit to the Duck Commander warehouse, Willie and the guys are fired up to spend the day hanging out with the professional racecar driver.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Season Eve
programme.desc: Tomorrow is the opening day of Duck Season and the men are carrying on their tradition of camping on the Robertson Land the night before.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Can't Hardly Weight
programme.desc: Gearing up for his high school reunion, Willie realizes that he could stand to lose a few pounds. Jase and Korie set out to help him drop some weight and the strenuous battle of man-versus-waistline begins.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Shot Thru The Heart
programme.desc: The guys learn from Willie that John Luke is going through his first break up. In order to make him feel better the men decide to take John Luke hunting for nutria, and impart their relationship wisdom.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Here Lizard, Lizard
programme.desc: When the warehouse guys new office pet (a lizard) escapes, they try all they can in order to catch the wily reptile. Proving impossible, the guys finally decide to bring in the big guns to help trap it--Mr. Phil Robertson.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hallu-Si-Nations
programme.desc: With Willie out-of-town, the guys decide to flake off work and leave to brush up the duck blinds on the Robertson land. While out, Si believes he spots what he thinks is the infamous 'Black Panther'.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Let's Go Hunting, Deer
programme.desc: Willie and Jase invite Korie and Missy along on a deer hunt because they believe their wives have become too "city." At Phil and Kay's, Miss Kay decides she wants to frame Phil's original blueprints from the first duck call he created.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Tickets To The Fun Show
programme.desc: During Duck Commander's "Casino Night" party, Si Robertson wins the grand prize of $2000 and decides to buy a massage chair for the duck call room. The massage chair becomes a detriment to the workflow, so Willie takes Si around for a replacement prize.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Be a Lady
programme.desc: Sadie, has a homecoming dance coming up, but Willie doesn't approve of her dress selection. Willie decides to take Sadie out to find her a new "appropriate" dress.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Monster Spider: A massive spider's web takes over a park, and experts investigate if this monster's trap could capture a human. A tree spurts out thick red blood.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Doomsday at Yellowstone: Scientists seek to explain the unexplainable while investigating blue fire near Yellowstone, turkeys circling a dead cat and a Gettysberg ghost.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Freak of the Deep: Deep-sea explorers discover a monster thousands of feet deep in the ocean, killer fog threatens a US town, and a truck spontaneously explodes on a freeway.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Curse of the Mayan Treasure: An explorer at a Mayan sacrificial site is attacked by an underwater fog, thousands of fish jump to their deaths, and a UFO is spotted in LA skies.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Beast That Ate Jaws: A shark is eaten by a mysterious predator, and experts wonder if a prehistoric monster is to blame. A sinister sight is filmed near Russia's Area 51.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Omen of Blood River: A river of blood washes through a city in Bangladesh, an ominous noise scares a Canadian town and a giant hole appears in the clouds above California.

programme.title: Battle of Britain
programme.desc: To commemorate the 70th Anniversary of the Battle of Britain Ewan and Colin McGregor take to the skies to discover how; against the odds, the RAF inflicted Hitler's first defeat of WW2.

programme.title: Pearl Harbor: A Day of Infamy
programme.desc: December 7th, 1941 Pearl Harbor was attacked by the Empire of Japan. Now the people who were there share their memories of that fateful day.

programme.title: Stories from the Blitz
programme.desc: Stories from the Blitz features new interviews with those who were there and amazing archive footage of the devastation, and of human courage and sheer will to survive one of Britain's darkest periods in History.

programme.title: Boy Soldiers Of World War II
programme.desc: In Britain during World War Two, thousands of men over the age of 18 were conscripted into the army, but among them were hundreds of underage boys, some as young as 15, who lied about their age so that they could join up.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: B24: The Liberator proved its worth in WWII over Europe and the Pacific, modeled after the B-17, delivering blows to the Axis.

programme.title: Hitler & Churchill: The Eagle & The Lion
programme.desc: The Old Lion vs. the German Eagle. This clash of titans waged at the highest level of power dominated the last century. And leads us to posit a chilling contingency to history: what would Europe be now had the players been different?

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered: A New Cold War
programme.desc: A New Cold War: How will the new Cold War, involving the US, Russia, the UK and China, play out? Will trade and cyber warfare be the worst of it or is military war inevitable?

programme.title: How To Lose A War
programme.desc: In our final episode, we examine how each Axis Power had troubles on their own home front, leading to disaster in the final days of the war.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Discover the true story of a German Luftwaffe pilot in World War II who risked his own life to save the crew of an American B-17 bomber.

programme.title: The Sound And The Fury
programme.desc: On September 1, 1939, Germany’s invasion of Poland throws the world into war. No longer a European affair, the conflict spreads: in the Pacific, Japan - at war with China since 1937 - accelerates its imperial ambitions.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Sharks and Recreation
programme.desc: With only three weeks left in the North Atlantic Bluefin Tuna season, the pressure begins to take a toll on the captains.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: The Mighty Bite
programme.desc: With only two weeks left in the fishing season and the price of blue fin tuna on the rise, the Gloucester captains race to bring in record hauls.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: The Reckoning
programme.desc: There is only one week of fishing left in the North Atlantic Bluefin Tuna season and the Gloucester fleet is set for a final showdown in a bid to catch the last monster tuna of the year.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Head To Tail (Special)
programme.desc: It's a look back at the best moments from two seasons of Wicked Tuna, featuring the wildest catches and fiercest rivalries, plus a sneak peek at the upcoming third season.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: First Blood
programme.desc: It's the first day of the new Bluefin tuna fishing season in Gloucester, Massachusetts, and every captain in the fleet wants to the land the coveted first tuna of the year.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Anchor Anger
programme.desc: The rivalry between captains Dave Carraro and Tyler McLaughlin intensifies, while other boats in the fleet push to bring new mates up tospeed in the North Atlantic Bluefin fishing season.

programme.title: Christmas Wars

programme.title: Customer Wars

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Skor plans to flatten Megalopolis with a massive, 60-story tall rock, but he suddenly has no one to help him push the rock down his mountain. His normally-subservient army of Links has run off in disgust over being fed the wrong meal at lunchtime.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: The Posse is battling with Skor over a crystal near the local airport, when the crystal slips out of both their hands and flies into a suitcase being loaded onto a passenger jet. The Posse springs into action to recover the crystal.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Brave Battler
programme.desc: With LINK VRAINS under attack, it’s up to the flashiest duelist ever to save the day – Shima!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: After an attempt by Skor to destroy the dinos goes horribly wrong and lands him in a vat of quick-hardening chocolate, Skrap convinces Skor that what he needs is a vacation. After all, evil-doing week in and week out can take a lot out of you.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Ace goes undercover as a criminal jet pilot in order to infiltrate an underground racing league, headed up by a crime syndicate known as the Scarlet Circle, in order to get close to the elusive head of the syndicate - The Red Baroness.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: As the KFDP headquarters becomes more modernized, Kane feels like he’s losing touch with his dino roots. So he announces to the others that he’s moving out… into a rustic cave on the outskirts of town just like back in the good old prehistoric days.

programme.title: Street Sharks

programme.title: Magi-Nation

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Skor needs a brain to complete his latest creation, Frankendino. But the only brain that’s good enough is Edgar’s, so Skor hands Skrap a serrated brain spoon and sends him off to fetch it.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Up the Volume

programme.title: M.A.S.K.

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Arthur: Brain's Brain/Brain Sees Stars
programme.desc: Brain's Easter egg hunts are notoriously hard. But this year's might prove impossible if Brain can't remember where he hid the final egg. Then, crazy coincidences have prevented Brain from seeing a meteor shower in person.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W., All Fired Up/I'd Rather Read It Myself
programme.desc: When Ms. Morgan announces they'll be having a fire drill during preschool, D.W. wants no part of it--it sounds way too scary. Then, the Tibble Twins are better than D.W. at just about everything.

programme.title: Arthur: Portrait of the Artist as a Young Tibble/The Secret Guardians
programme.desc: The Tibble Twins start a new business to earn enough money to buy the coveted Krummy Kreepy Kastle. When and friends stumble upon a beautiful woodland oasis right by their homes, they vow to tell no one and keep it as their secret spot.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W., The Picky Eater/Buster And The Daredevils
programme.desc: It's wrong to call D.W. a picky eater. The only things she won't eat are vegetables, fish, fruit, and meat. Then, Arthur and Buster meet two really cool kids and, naturally, are utterly dazzled. How far will Buster go to be cool?

programme.title: Arthur: Francine Redecorates/Arthur The Loser
programme.desc: Francine and Catherine are very different, but it’s no fun if you're two sisters sharing the same room. Then, there's only one thing in life Arthur really wants—to win! But he just can't stop losing. Is it really so wrong to cheat a little?

programme.title: Arthur: The Election/Francine Goes To War
programme.desc: There’s a mock election in Mr. Ratburn's class, but Muffy takes it seriously. How far will she go to win? Francine can't believe it. She gets in trouble no matter what she does -- thanks to the new nosy next-door neighbor!

programme.title: Arthur: Baby Kate and the Imaginary Mystery/Strangers on a Train
programme.desc: Nadine, D.W.'s imaginary friend is missing. Sue Ellen can't wait to travel on an old-fashioned train, but once onboard, she discovers that the train is not as spectacular as she had hoped–until she finds a mysterious diary.

programme.title: Arthur: The Contest/Prove It
programme.desc: What would happen if Arthur and his friends entered a contest to write stories for their favorite TV show? Then, look out world, D.W. has discovered science! But D.W.'s new-found passion proves that a little knowledge is a dangerous thing.

programme.title: Arthur: Summer Stories
programme.desc: It’s time to celebrate summer! Join Arthur as he takes a family vacation and visits Grandpa Dave’s farm. Then, Arthur, D.W. and Buster spend a few days at the beach where they discover the simple joys of building sandcastles!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Riff can’t find his drumsticks anywhere. Barney helps Baby Bop explore many different jobs.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ learns a lesson about bragging when, playing superhero CAPTAIN PICKLES, he tells everyone that he can leap over the moon!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: BJ's Really Cool House
programme.desc: Although the park may be a home away from home for Barney and his friends, BJ wants to build his own "totally awesome, completely cool" house. While BJ works on his house, Barney and the children learn all about houses and homes.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop isn’t satisfied with the things she can do. When a wheelchair-bound child is unintentionally neglected by his friends, Barney steps in to help.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Caring Hearts
programme.desc: A special project provides the perfect opportunity for Barney, Baby Bop and BJ to learn about love and relationships the "heart" way as they make a "Friends and Family Tree."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Coming on Strong
programme.desc: Exercise is good for everybody and every body! From running to touching toes to jumping jacks to some favorite songs and games, Barney and his friends grow strong while having fun with exercise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Moving Along
programme.desc: Barney plays a fun game of "Simon Says" with Baby Bop and BJ in the park. They demonstrate how to move their bodies through dancing and playing games.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ and Baby Bop decide to get Halloween celebrations underway with a costume party. When Ramon loses his trick-or-treat candy, BJ shares his treats with Ramon.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham's The Cars That Drove Us

programme.title: Jeff Dunham's The Cars That Drove Us

programme.title: The Exes

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995
programme.desc: When a robot named Adam is accused of killing his own creator, it's up to a civil liberties attorney (Leonard Nimoy) to defend him. Is Adam capable of murder? Or did a military retrofit of the docile robot turn him into a ruthless killing machine?

programme.title: The Outer Limits: 1995

programme.title: My Ghost Story

programme.desc: In 2015, a 69-year-old man is found stabbed in his home. At the scene, forensics uncover a receipt from a nearby cashpoint. CCTV at the bank reveals a woman using the victim's card to withdraw cash. Who is she? And how does she relate to the murder?

programme.title: Murdertown: Pontypridd
programme.desc: In 2014, a woman goes missing after a night drinking in the local pubs. CCTV finds her walking home arm in arm with another drinker. He just happens to be a former butcher. Police soon realise he has put his trade to use by dismembering his victim.

programme.title: Murdertown: Tamworth
programme.desc: In 1994, a gang kill a woman who manages a building society and tie up her husband, before robbing the building society of large sums of cash. But despite making a tearful plea for help, the injured husband’s story doesn’t tally.

programme.title: Murdertown: Watford
programme.desc: In 2000, a young woman is stabbed to death while walking her dog in a picturesque park in the town. A suspect is seen running semi-naked across the M1 motorway. It takes detectives weeks to identify the killer as a local man called Leon Amos.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Faria Khan
programme.desc: After leaving an arranged marriage she claimed was abusive, Faria Khan could have moved on. But she wouldn’t be satisfied until estranged husband Nawajid was dead. And she knew exactly who to ask to help her.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Steven McDowell
programme.desc: Steven and Crystal McDowell were never a good match, and for years after they split, Steven deluded himself that reconciliation was just around the corner. Then, Crystal disappeared during a deadly hurricane. Was she the victim of a natural disaster?

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Glyn Dix
programme.desc: Glyn Dix met Hazel while he was serving a life term for a brutal rape and murder. After convincing her he had been framed, the couple fell for one another. But five years after his release from prison, Glyn finally showed Hazel his true colours.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Colleagues and customers at the pharmacy owned by Jessica and Mitesh Patel recall how abusive, controlling and sometimes violent Mitesh was towards his wife. But they had no idea that he was hatching a plan to brutally end the marriage.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: Carole and Harry Jarvis were married for over 30 years. But he met a new woman, heiress Rita Heyster, and decided it was time to move on. In a bizarre, twisted scheme, Harry and Rita plotted to murder Carole and cover up her death.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
programme.desc: William Walker was a firefighter and paramedic with a reputation for helping strangers. Little wonder that debt-ridden Uloma Curry was attracted to him. Within weeks of their wedding she was plotting to get her hands on his cash.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Pointing to Murder
programme.desc: A withered corpse is found half-buried in a field beside a theme park. Too decayed to identify properly, detectives have only the leathery fingers of one hand to work from.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Good Doctor
programme.desc: A single mother in Saskatchewan visits a hospital in the midst of a panic attack. Her doctor sedates her. Then, she is certain, he rapes her.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Sweet Killer
programme.desc: An aging hippy is going through bad times. When he's found dead in his home with a gut of antifreeze, it's a closed case. Then a shocking confession reveals a maelstrom of lies and a story of cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: The Stranger
programme.desc: Bored at home, a retiree takes a job at a 24-hour convenience store. On her third night at work a customer finds her behind the register in a pool of blood.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Hunted
programme.desc: A real estate broker doesn’t return home. Her family can't find her. But police do - dead in a closet in her office. The DNA on her body is tested against 150 men. No match. The case runs cold.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Predator
programme.desc: The phone rings in the middle of the night. On the other end of the line, the sounds of an attack. The couple that receive the call rush to its source and discover a horrific scene.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Evil Eyes
programme.desc: A dead woman is found in a park. It’s learned the deceased is a divorced landlady of a group home for transients. The suspect list is long; the clues to follow are few.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Lust
programme.desc: One morning in the stairwell of a Toronto building, a guard makes a grotesque discovery: the dead body of a Filipina nanny. It's clear she's been savagely murdered and raped.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Model Killer
programme.desc: A gorgeous young model is bludgeoned to death in her downtown apartment. The wounds to her head are so severe police initially presume she's been shot.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Dead End
programme.desc: A young couple hits the road, hitchhiking across North America. Free spirits keen to explore the world. When the young woman stops phoning home, her father is alarmed.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Bull's Eye

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Klink reluctantly falls in with two other Kommandants plotting against Burkhalter. Burkhalter finds out. Now Hogan must step in before Stalag 13 loses Klink the military way - by firing squad.

programme.desc: Hogan's operation is known to a Gestapo man who wants to trade his knowledge for $1,000,000 in diamonds, but can he be trusted?

programme.desc: Efforts to aid a Nazi general's assassination plan hit a snag when Schultz unknowingly activates a time bomb meant for the Führer.

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: Touched
programme.desc: When Harvard psychiatrist John Mack approached filmmaker Laurel Chiten asking her to make a movie about encounters with aliens, she thought he was crazy. But after meeting some of the so-called experiencers she was intruiged.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES
programme.desc: Scientists, engineers, military, and intelligence agencies push boundaries with unlimited budgets and secrecy. This episode unveils known (or suspected) top-secret projects.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: THE MYSTERY AND CULTS OF MUMMIES
programme.desc: Unlock the mystery of mummification from ancient Egypt to present. Explore its fascination and quest for Queen Nefertiti's mummy. Where did 70 million ancient mummies disappear? Delve into Latin American mummy legends.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MEDICAL MYSTERIES
programme.desc: Medical history is rife with jaw-dropping oddities, miraculous recoveries, and unsolved mysteries. Beyond shock value, these cases showcase human resilience and the vast unknowns in understanding the body and mind.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET PROJECTS AND OPERATIONS
programme.desc: Imagine a world with open use of resources from secret government projects. Little is known about these black ops. They hold mysteries and consequences, challenging democratic ideals. History reveals human experimentation, contrary to official claims.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Waterspouts
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, George uses a motorboat, a van and small plane to get up close to waterspouts - the nautical cousins of tornadoes. He also meets some of the 25 million alligators that live in the Sunshine State.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Blizzards
programme.desc: Winter weather can be even more dangerous than thunderstorms and where else to explore the big chill than at the summit of Mount Washington which has the reputation as being the place with the "Worst weather in the world".

programme.title: Angry Planet: Indonesia
programme.desc: In Indonesia George visits massive Komodo Dragons, explores steaming sulfur vents of Papandayan, visits villagers whose town got swept away by Sidoarjo Mudflow, and tackles his toughest climb to date - the very active Semeru Volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Desert Monsoon
programme.desc: Enlisting the help of fellow storm chaser R.J. Evans, George heads to Arizona to photograph spectacular lightning storms triggered by the desert monsoon season. R.J. however is known as "America's noisiest athiest" for his free-thought radio show.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Fin Del Mundo
programme.desc: Although he admits to not being much of a sailor, George climbs aboard a sailing vessel to explore the extreme southern tip of South America (Fin Del Mundo aka the "End of the World").

programme.title: Angry Planet: Under Pressure
programme.desc: After submarine exploration of Grand Cayman Island, George dives with Canadian Armed Forces' experimental deep pressure unit. With explorers Chris Harvey-Clark and Phil Nuytten he investigates newly discovered "Black Smoker" deep sea vents.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane
programme.desc: Linking up with legendary storm chaser Jim Leonard, George flies to Jamaica to track Hurricane Dean. George then travels to Nova Scotia to experience the even bigger turmoil of Hurricane Noel at the iconic Maritime village of Peggy's Cove.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Big Bang - The First Three Seconds
programme.desc: 13.7 billion years ago there was absolutely nothing. No space, no time, and no matter. Naked Science follows the first three seconds of creation when a speck of light appeared from nothing and rapidly expanded into an enormous Universe.

programme.title: Naked Science: Magnetic Earth
programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists give true insider's perspective to the space race of the late 1960's.

programme.title: How to Colonize the Stars
programme.desc: Leading physicists and engineers in the field of interstellar travel discuss some of the deep issues we face when considering the problem of sending spacecraft to other stars. Will mankind ever truly become a space fairing civilization?

programme.title: Hunting the Edge of Space: The Mystery of the Milky Way
programme.desc: The Mystery of the Milky Way chronicles the history of telescopes, from Galileo's refractor to Newton's reflector and beyond. This episode also looks at recent missions, including the Herschel Space Observatory's examination of the Milky Wa

programme.title: Hunting the Edge of Space: The Ever Expanding Universe
programme.desc: The Ever-Expanding Universe investigates a battery of high-tech telescopes that are joining the Hubble Space Telescope on its quest to unlock the secrets of our universe, a cosmos almost incomprehensible in its size, age and violence.

programme.title: In the Shadow of the Moon
programme.desc: Between 1968 and 1972, the world watched in awe each time an American spacecraft voyaged to the Moon. Only 12 men walked upon its surface and they remain the only human beings to have stood on another world.

programme.title: Wild Germany: Rivers and Lakes
programme.desc: Water is the beginning of all life - including the wildlife of Germany. The episode Rivers and Lakes takes the viewer on a journey through the water world of Wild Germany.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The environment of southern Europe and North Africa is the perfect territory for oak trees, huge trees whose branches may be home to more than 300 species of animals.

programme.title: Wildest Islands
programme.desc: Islands can be home to the most extreme examples of life, the wildest of people and the most dramatic landscapes. Natural selection fuels evolution in the most dramatic way

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: History repeats itself with terrifying consequences for Swift while Flint faces the Ubuntu gang and her first real test.

programme.title: Wildest Europe
programme.desc: From mountain to sea, and from scorching desert to Arctic tundra, Europe’s great wilderness contains a stunning and surprising explosion of life. Wildest Europe reveals the incredible beauty and diversity of wildlife in Europe’s major natural habitats.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Vietnam - Phoenix from the Ashes
programme.desc: Vietnam is the easternmost country on the Indochina peninsula in Southeast Asia. Around ten percent of the world’s species live here. But less than 50 years ago, things were very different. Vietnam’s forests were decimated.

programme.title: Great British Royal Ships: Her Majesty's Yacht Britannia
programme.desc: A vessel that has been a floating palace and a rescue vessel.

programme.title: Great British Royal Ships: RMS Queen Mary
programme.desc: The fascinating tale of the landmark ship, the Queen Mary.

programme.title: London: 2000 Years Of History
programme.desc: Dan Jones discovers the ruins of Roman Londinium.

programme.title: London: 2000 Years Of History
programme.desc: Dr Suzannah Lipscomb enters Henry VIII's pleasure palace.

programme.title: London: 2000 Years Of History
programme.desc: The hosts arrive as the Great Fire almost wipes the city out.

programme.title: London: 2000 Years Of History
programme.desc: How the Crossrail will propel the capital into the future.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony explores the other Hawaii, the one that those eight million tourists don’t see when they come to the islands. He discovers the fabric of a true cultural mixing pot.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony visits Andalucia during Semana Santa – Holy Week – a time filled with great pageantry and excitement. Tony’s longtime DP, Zach Zamboni, currently lives in Granada with his fiancée, Fuen Sanchez.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Roy Choi’s food reflects a new American idea of "natural" fusion. Bourdain examines the meeting point of Asian, Mexican, Latino culture in modern LA.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Israel, the West Bank and Gaza are three locations Tony has never travelled to before.The intense and fraught relationship between these places is hard to understand but Tony will attempt to delve into the lives of those who live here, through their food.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Sicily isn’t about fast food or the fast life. It’s about savoring family, life, food and making the most of what you have. Tony will go in search of those foods and their producers and eat his way around the island.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Few countries have a more contentious relationship with the United States than Iran. In this episode we heads to this Shia Muslim nation to explore a country few Americans have visited since the 80s.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Punjab, India
programme.desc: Tony dives into the ever-changing state of Punjab, India. Starting off his journey in Amritsar, Tony is immediately struck by the chaos around him.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Big Island, Small Town
programme.desc: Dog has problems when he goes to a small town on the Big Island of Hawaii to track down a fugitive. In a small town, everyone knows everyone else's business, making it impossible for Dog to hunt in secret.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Jonah is Missing
programme.desc: Dog and the team search for Jonah, a violent and desperate criminal facing?20 years in jail. The trail goes stale when Dog runs into evasive family members and an angry girlfriend.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Suga On My Cuffs
programme.desc: Dog and the team go after Kandice, AKA Candy, AKA Suga, a stripper who didn't make her court appearance. They convince one of her friends to lure her to a supermarket parking lot, where Dog has an easy time fetching the fetching young thing. But then

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Mothers and Daughters
programme.desc: Dog and Beth take on the heartbreaking case of Rachel. Rachel's mom bailed her out of jail on condition that she go to AA meetings and stick to a curfew. But Rachel hasn't kept up her end of the bargain, and now her mother wants her back to jail.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Dog Is Smokin'
programme.desc: Dog is trying to cut down on smoking, but it's not easy with his high-stress job. Today, he must track down Walter, a fugitive who believes that the Canadian military has planted a chip in his tooth that allows him to communicate with his van.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: You Snooze, You Lose
programme.desc: Beth designs a luxurious new office for the team and shows it off to Dog, but the bounty hunter is more interested in starting his hunt for Federico, who's been revoked for missing court dates and generally running amuck.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Dog goes on his a case, which involves Bobby, who's been on parole for three months pending sentencing on an assault charge. Bobby's girlfriend wants him back in jail, and Dog agrees that the Bobby needs to be behind bars.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Moms & Dads
programme.desc: Dog pursues two people whose lives have been ruined by drugs. First, he tracks Mae, who traded a good home and loving family for an ice pipe. The next case involves Jude. His addiction has caused his pregnant wife to fear for her safety.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Do Unto Others
programme.desc: Dog is a rough, tough bounty hunter, but he's a softie at heart, as the two cases in this episode show.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: A Helping Hand
programme.desc: Dog answers two very different calls for help from women. In the first case, he helps Leland protect Maria, who's being threatened by her violent husband. Then Dog visit a young woman who's dying and has asked to see Dog as one of her final wishes.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Take Your Daughter to Work Day
programme.desc: Baby Lyssa, who's just turned 18 and is studying to become a bondsman, gets to do some on-the-job training when she helps Dog hunt for Wayne, who's run out on a bond for second-degree assault.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Be a Lady
programme.desc: Sadie, has a homecoming dance coming up, but Willie doesn't approve of her dress selection. Willie decides to take Sadie out to find her a new "appropriate" dress.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Ring Around the Redneck
programme.desc: Though Jase lost his wedding ring long ago, Missy decides it's time for Jase to replace it with a new one. When Jase is duck hunting, once again, he loses the ring.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Bass Man Standing
programme.desc: The Duck Commander team is invited to unveil a wing of the local Bass Pro shop that will feature their products.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Battle of the Brothers
programme.desc: Willie's suspicious behavior from dyeing his beard to buying a new motorcycle, makes Jase come to the conclusion that Willie is having a mid-life crisis.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Aloha, Robertsons!
programme.desc: After all their hard work, Willie decides to take the entire Robertson clan on a family vacation to Hawaii. But Willie's daily itinerary for the trip gets constantly undermined by the family's desire to participate in their own island activities.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Till Duck Do Us Part
programme.desc: Everyone comes together to plan a surprise wedding for Phil and Kay, setting up a special ceremony on their land, which the whole family attends. Si distracts Phil and Kay by taking them on a "trip down memory lane".

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: So You Think You Can Date?
programme.desc: After discovering that Martin has to miss a Friday night poker game for a date, the guys (as well as their wives) take turns figuring out the best way to coach him for romance. When babysitting for Jep and Jessica turns into a rocky road.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hot Tub Grime Machine
programme.desc: The guys realize that Godwin has never really asked them for a favor, even though they are all always helping each other out. Godwin asks them to help him get a hot tub, so they set out to help him pick one up.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: A-Jase-ent Living
programme.desc: Due to construction at Jase and Missy's house, Willie and Korie invite Jase and the kids to live with them while Missy is out of town visiting friends. Then, what begins in the duck call room as a series of "Gentlemen's Bets".

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Omen of Blood River: A river of blood washes through a city in Bangladesh, an ominous noise scares a Canadian town and a giant hole appears in the clouds above California.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Return of the Ice Monster: A giant ice creature is spotted in Alaska, a jet has a close encounter with a UFO, and evidence of a lightning strike invites paranormal speculation.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: The Incident at Area 51: Near Area 51, a police convoy is filmed transporting an alien-looking craft in the dead of night. Strange beams of light are reported in Argentina.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: America's Loch Ness Monster: A Loch Ness Monster-esque creature is recorded in America, experts analyse evidence of a giant scaly egg, and birds are vaporized by a death ray.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Gateway to Hell: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert, a deadly shark lurks on a golf course, and a giant white blob threatens a town in California.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Wrath of Thor: A mysterious flame appears in the Norwegian wilderness and video footage shows a ghostly figure stalking people in Japan, confirming a local legend.

programme.title: Boy Soldiers Of World War II
programme.desc: In Britain during World War Two, thousands of men over the age of 18 were conscripted into the army, but among them were hundreds of underage boys, some as young as 15, who lied about their age so that they could join up.

programme.title: USS Indianapolis: The Legacy
programme.desc: Experience this groundbreaking film, the story of the greatest sea disaster in U.S. Navy History, told for the first time by only the men who lived it.

programme.title: Field of Valor
programme.desc: It was becoming clear in 1939 that direct U.S. involvement in World War Two was only a matter of time. Training approximately 500 to 750 pilots a year, the United States Army Air Corps was severely outmatched by the warring powers of Europe and Asia.

programme.title: Destroy Paris: Hitler's Secret Plan
programme.desc: By 1944, as the Reich was crumbling, Hitler gave detailed orders for the destruction of Paris. He wanted the monuments and all the bridges mined and the city reduced to a 'pile of rubble'.

programme.title: The Sound And The Fury
programme.desc: On September 1, 1939, Germany’s invasion of Poland throws the world into war. No longer a European affair, the conflict spreads: in the Pacific, Japan - at war with China since 1937 - accelerates its imperial ambitions.

programme.title: How To Lose A War
programme.desc: Explore how the Axis Powers' ruthless, brutal occupation and exploitation of other countries contributed to their demise.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft: The Spitfire: A British Fighter Legend
programme.desc: Unusually, we look at the later, clipped wing version flown by Czech refugee pilots in the UK during WW2. We get close-up with a beautifully restored example, fly her and unearth long unseen test and operational footage.

programme.title: The Sound And The Fury
programme.desc: At Stalingrad, the German 6th Army is destroyed by the Soviets. It’s the first major defeat of the Third Reich, which marks the beginning of its decline. Elsewhere, after the American victory at Guadalcanal, Japan loses the initiative in the Pacific.

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: An adrenaline-fuelled dash through nazi-occupied Europe known as ‘The Flight of the Crow’, a death-defying escape across the world’s most perilous mountain range, and the largest escape of world war two - by men who chose death over dishonour.

programme.title: How To Lose A War
programme.desc: Explore how the seeds of Japan's defeat in 1945 were sown a decade earlier with the occupation of Manchuria in China.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Anchor Anger
programme.desc: The rivalry between captains Dave Carraro and Tyler McLaughlin intensifies, while other boats in the fleet push to bring new mates up tospeed in the North Atlantic Bluefin fishing season.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Bluefin Beatdown

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Harpoon Hellraising
programme.desc: It's week four of the North Atlantic Blue Fin Tuna Season and oneveteran Gloucester captain has yet to catch his first fish, while two rival harpoon boats go head to head.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Go Hard or Go Home

programme.title: Bent Rods and Broken Hearts
programme.desc: It's week six of the North Atlantic Bluefin tuna fishing season and the Gloucester fleet's underdogs struggle to turn around their seasons.
